title: Phase 2: Wire config into runners and CLI
description: Wire OrchestratorConfig into runner.py and cli.py. Both runners accept config and backend in __init__. cli.py loads config via load_config() and resolves backend from --runner flag > env var > config.default_runner. ClaudeCodeAgentRunner resolves per-agent-type allowed tools via config.allowed_tools_for(). Remove _RUNNERS registry dict and hardcoded binary names, CLI flags, --allowedTools string, default runner.
acceptance_criteria: cli.py make_services() calls load_config(root) and threads config through
Backend resolution order: --runner flag > $ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER > config.default_runner
Both runners accept config and backend in __init__
CodexAgentRunner builds cmd from self.backend.binary + self.backend.flags
ClaudeCodeAgentRunner resolves tools via config.allowed_tools_for('claude', agent_type)
run_bead() passes agent_type=bead.agent_type to _exec_json()
propose_plan() passes agent_type='planner' to _exec_json()
Backward compatibility: default_config() used if config not passed to constructor
_RUNNERS registry dict removed from cli.py
All existing tests pass

handoff_summary.completed: All acceptance criteria met: cli.py make_services() calls load_config(root); backend resolution order is --runner > env > config.default_runner; both runners accept config+backend; CodexAgentRunner builds cmd from self.backend.binary + self.backend.flags; ClaudeCodeAgentRunner resolves tools via config.allowed_tools_for(); run_bead() passes agent_type=bead.agent_type; propose_plan() passes agent_type='planner'; backward compat via default_config() fallback; _RUNNERS registry removed; all 191 tests pass.
handoff_summary.risks: Downstream code that imports _RUNNERS from cli.py will break (no such usages found). Tests that mock make_services will continue to work since the signature is unchanged.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run followup test/docs/review beads for B0104.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Phase 3 (scheduler/prompts/skills config wiring) will touch scheduler.py which imports from runner.py but no signature conflicts expected.

conflict_risks: Phase 3 (scheduler/prompts/skills config wiring) will touch scheduler.py which imports from runner.py but no signature conflicts expected.

execution_history.summary: Wired OrchestratorConfig into runner.py and cli.py. Both CodexAgentRunner and ClaudeCodeAgentRunner now accept config and backend in __init__ (with backward-compatible default_config() fallback). CLI loads config via load_config(root) and resolves backend via --runner > $ORCHESTRATOR_RUNNER > config.default_runner. ClaudeCodeAgentRunner resolves per-agent-type allowed tools via config.allowed_tools_for(). Removed _RUNNERS registry dict and all hardcoded binary names, CLI flags, and allowed-tools strings. All 191 existing tests pass.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
